# RAGWire

RAGWire is a production-grade Python RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) toolkit for document ingestion and retrieval. Install with `pip install ragwire`. It loads documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, TXT, MD), splits them, extracts structured metadata via LLM, stores embeddings in Qdrant with hybrid dense+sparse search, and retrieves results with optional metadata filtering. The `RAGWire` class is the single entry point for all operations.

- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/ragwire/
- GitHub: https://github.com/laxmimerit/ragwire
- Docs: https://laxmimerit.github.io/RAGWire/
- Full API reference (plain text): https://laxmimerit.github.io/RAGWire/llms-full.txt

---

## Installation

```bash
# Base install (Ollama embedding, local Qdrant)
pip install ragwire

# With OpenAI
pip install "ragwire[openai]"

# With HuggingFace
pip install "ragwire[huggingface]"

# With Google Gemini
pip install "ragwire[google]"

# With Groq
pip install "ragwire[groq]"

# With Anthropic
pip install "ragwire[anthropic]"

# Everything
pip install "ragwire[all]"

# Hybrid search sparse vectors (always install separately)
pip install fastembed
```

---

## Quick Start — Minimum Working Example

```python
from ragwire import RAGWire

# Initialize from config.yaml (see config.yaml section below)
rag = RAGWire("config.yaml")

# Ingest files — SHA256 deduplication, safe to re-run
stats = rag.ingest_documents(["data/Apple_10k_2025.pdf", "data/Microsoft_10k_2025.pdf"])
print(f"Processed: {stats['processed']}, Skipped: {stats['skipped']}, Chunks: {stats['chunks_created']}")

# Ingest a whole directory
stats = rag.ingest_directory("data/", recursive=True, extensions=[".pdf", ".docx"])

# Basic retrieval — returns list of LangChain Document objects
results = rag.retrieve("What is the total revenue?", top_k=5)
for doc in results:
    print(doc.page_content[:300])
    print(doc.metadata["company_name"])   # str, lowercased, e.g. "apple"
    print(doc.metadata["fiscal_year"])    # list[int], e.g. [2025]  ← NOT a plain int
    print(doc.metadata["file_name"])      # str, e.g. "Apple_10k_2025.pdf"

# Retrieval with explicit metadata filters (most common pattern)
results = rag.retrieve(
    "What is the net income?",
    top_k=5,
    filters={"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025}  # pass year as int, stored as [int]
)

# OR logic within a field: match any of the listed values
results = rag.retrieve(
    "Compare revenue",
    filters={"fiscal_year": [2023, 2024, 2025]}  # matches chunks from any of these years
)

# Agent-controlled filtering (recommended for AI agents)
filters = rag.extract_filters("Apple's revenue in 2025")
# filters → {"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025} or None
results = rag.retrieve("Apple's revenue in 2025", filters=filters)
```

---

## config.yaml — Complete Reference

Copy `config.example.yaml` to `config.yaml`. All sections shown below. The four sections `embeddings`, `llm`, `vectorstore`, and `retriever` are required.

```yaml
# =================================================================
# Document Loader
# =================================================================
loader:
  extensions: [".pdf", ".docx", ".xlsx", ".pptx", ".txt", ".md"]

# =================================================================
# Text Splitter
# =================================================================
splitter:
  chunk_size: 10000
  chunk_overlap: 2000
  strategy: "markdown"   # "markdown" | "recursive"

# =================================================================
# Embedding Model — pick ONE provider block
# =================================================================
embeddings:
  # --- Ollama (local, no API key) ---
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3-embedding:0.6b"
  base_url: "http://localhost:11434"

  # --- OpenAI ---
  # provider: "openai"
  # model: "text-embedding-3-small"
  # (reads OPENAI_API_KEY from environment automatically)

  # --- HuggingFace (local) ---
  # provider: "huggingface"
  # model_name: "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

  # --- Google ---
  # provider: "google"
  # model: "models/embedding-001"
  # (reads GOOGLE_API_KEY from environment automatically)

  # --- FastEmbed (local, CPU-friendly) ---
  # provider: "fastembed"
  # model: "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"

# =================================================================
# LLM — used for metadata extraction on every ingested document
# =================================================================
llm:
  # --- Ollama (local) ---
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3.5:9b"
  base_url: "http://localhost:11434"
  num_ctx: 16384         # Context window — must fit your largest chunk

  # --- OpenAI ---
  # provider: "openai"
  # model: "gpt-4o-mini"

  # --- Google Gemini ---
  # provider: "google"
  # model: "gemini-2.0-flash"

  # --- Groq ---
  # provider: "groq"
  # model: "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"

  # --- Anthropic ---
  # provider: "anthropic"
  # model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"

# =================================================================
# Custom Metadata (optional)
# =================================================================
# metadata:
#   config_file: "metadata.yaml"   # Path to custom metadata YAML
#                                  # If omitted, built-in financial fields are used:
#                                  # company_name, doc_type, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year

# =================================================================
# Vector Store (Qdrant)
# =================================================================
vectorstore:
  url: "http://localhost:6333"          # Local Qdrant
  # url: "https://your-cluster.qdrant.io"  # Qdrant Cloud
  # api_key: "${QDRANT_API_KEY}"           # Env var substitution syntax
  # url: "./qdrant_storage"                # File-based local storage
  collection_name: "financial_docs"
  use_sparse: true       # Enables hybrid search. Requires: pip install fastembed
  force_recreate: false  # WARNING: true WIPES the collection. Set once, then back to false.

# =================================================================
# Retriever
# =================================================================
retriever:
  search_type: "hybrid"   # "similarity" | "mmr" | "hybrid"
                          # "hybrid" requires use_sparse: true + pip install fastembed
  top_k: 5
  auto_filter: false      # If true, LLM extracts metadata filters from every query automatically.
                          # For agents: keep false and use rag.extract_filters(query) instead.

# =================================================================
# Logging
# =================================================================
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  console_output: true
  colored: true
  # log_file: "logs/rag.log"
```

**Environment variable substitution:** Use `${VAR_NAME}` anywhere in config.yaml. Values are read from environment or `.env` file (loaded automatically via python-dotenv).

---

## API — RAGWire Class

```python
from ragwire import RAGWire
rag = RAGWire(config_path: str)
```

### Ingestion

```python
# Ingest a list of files
stats = rag.ingest_documents(file_paths: list[str]) -> IngestStats
# IngestStats keys: total (int), processed (int), skipped (int), failed (int),
#                   chunks_created (int), errors (list[IngestError])
# IngestError keys: file (str), error (str)

# Ingest a directory
stats = rag.ingest_directory(
    directory: str,
    recursive: bool = False,
    extensions: list[str] | None = None  # default: from config loader.extensions
) -> IngestStats  # same shape as ingest_documents()
```

### Retrieval

```python
# Standard retrieval (similarity, MMR, or hybrid — controlled by config)
results = rag.retrieve(
    query: str,
    top_k: int | None = None,           # overrides config top_k
    filters: dict | None = None          # metadata filters
) -> list[Document]                      # LangChain Document objects

# Force hybrid search regardless of config
results = rag.hybrid_search(
    query: str,
    k: int = 5,
    filters: dict | None = None
) -> list[Document]

# Each Document has:
doc.page_content  # str — the chunk text
doc.metadata      # dict — see Metadata Schema section below
```

### Filtering

```python
# Extract metadata filters from a natural language query
filters = rag.extract_filters(query: str) -> dict | None
# Example: rag.extract_filters("Apple 2025 annual report")
# Returns: {"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025} or None

# Get formatted context for an agent to decide filters
context = rag.get_filter_context(
    query: str,
    limit: int = 50
) -> str  # Markdown-formatted string listing available fields and values

# Get unique stored values for specific fields
values = rag.get_field_values(
    fields: str | list[str],  # single field name or list
    limit: int = 50
) -> list | dict
# Example: rag.get_field_values("company_name") → ["apple", "microsoft", "google"]
# Example: rag.get_field_values(["company_name", "fiscal_year"]) → {"company_name": [...], "fiscal_year": [...]}

# List all filterable metadata fields in the collection
fields = rag.discover_metadata_fields() -> list[str]
# Returns: ["company_name", "doc_type", "fiscal_quarter", "fiscal_year", ...]

# Access filterable field names directly
fields = rag.filter_fields  # property → list[str]
```

### Metadata & Stats

```python
# Extract metadata from arbitrary text (without ingesting)
meta = rag.extract_metadata(text: str) -> dict

# Get collection statistics
stats = rag.get_stats() -> dict
# stats keys: collection_name (str), total_points (int),
#             vector_size (int), status (str)
```

---

## Metadata Schema

Every retrieved `doc.metadata` is a dict with these keys:

```python
doc.metadata = {
    # LLM-extracted (financial defaults — customizable via metadata.yaml)
    "company_name":   "apple",          # str, always lowercased
    "doc_type":       "10-K",           # str: "10-K", "10-Q", "8-K", etc.
    "fiscal_quarter": "q4",             # str: "q1", "q2", "q3", "q4", or None
    "fiscal_year":    [2025],           # list[int] — IMPORTANT: stored as a list, NOT plain int

    # File-level (auto-populated)
    "source":         "/data/Apple_10k_2025.pdf",
    "file_name":      "Apple_10k_2025.pdf",
    "file_type":      "pdf",
    "file_hash":      "a3f8...",        # SHA256 of file content

    # Chunk-level (auto-populated)
    "chunk_id":       "a3f8..._0",
    "chunk_hash":     "d7c2...",
    "chunk_index":    0,                # position of chunk within the document
    "total_chunks":   42,               # total chunks from this file
    "created_at":     "2026-04-19T...", # ISO format UTC timestamp
}
```

**Critical:** `fiscal_year` is stored as `list[int]` (e.g. `[2025]`), but when filtering, pass it as `int` (e.g. `filters={"fiscal_year": 2025}`). RAGWire handles the conversion internally.

**Custom metadata** (via metadata.yaml) adds extra keys alongside the above. File-level and chunk-level keys are always present.

---

## Filter Syntax — Three Modes

```python
# Mode 1: Explicit filters (most common — zero LLM overhead)
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"company_name": "apple"})
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025})
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"doc_type": "10-K", "fiscal_year": 2025})
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"fiscal_year": [2023, 2024, 2025]})  # OR logic within field

# Mode 2: auto_filter in config.yaml (set retriever.auto_filter: true)
rag.retrieve(query)  # LLM auto-extracts filters from the query on every call

# Mode 3: Agent-controlled (recommended for AI agents)
filters = rag.extract_filters(query)   # inspect/adjust before using
if filters:
    results = rag.retrieve(query, filters=filters)
else:
    results = rag.retrieve(query)      # no-filter fallback
```

**Filter rules:**
- String values must be lowercase — `"apple"` matches, `"Apple"` does not
- `fiscal_year` filter takes `int`, not `str` and not `list`
- List values use **OR logic within** the field: `{"fiscal_year": [2023, 2024]}` matches either year
- Multiple fields use **AND logic across** fields: `{"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025}` requires both
- Use `rag.get_field_values("company_name")` to discover valid stored values before filtering

---

## Agent Integration Pattern

Complete two-tool LangChain agent pattern:

```python
from typing import Optional
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain.tools import tool
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import InMemorySaver
from ragwire import RAGWire

rag = RAGWire("config.yaml")
rag.ingest_directory("data/")

@tool
def get_filter_context(query: str) -> str:
    """Get available metadata fields and filter suggestions for this query.
    Call before search_documents when the query involves specific metadata
    (company, year, document type). Skip for purely semantic queries."""
    return rag.get_filter_context(query)

@tool
def search_documents(query: str, filters: Optional[dict] = None) -> str:
    """Search the document knowledge base.
    Args:
        query: The search query
        filters: Optional metadata filters from get_filter_context. Pass {} to search without filtering."""
    results = rag.retrieve(query, top_k=5, filters=filters)
    if not results:
        return "No relevant documents found."
    chunks = []
    for doc in results:
        source = doc.metadata.get("file_name", "unknown")
        meta = {k: v for k, v in doc.metadata.items() if k != "file_name" and v not in (None, "", [])}
        header = f"[{source} | {meta}]"
        chunks.append(f"{header}\n{doc.page_content}")
    return "\n\n---\n\n".join(chunks)

model = ChatOllama(model="qwen3.5:9b", base_url="http://localhost:11434")
agent = create_agent(
    model=model,
    tools=[get_filter_context, search_documents],
    system_prompt=(
        "Always use search_documents to retrieve information. "
        "Use get_filter_context before search_documents when the query involves "
        "specific metadata (company, year, document type). "
        "Never answer from general knowledge. Always cite the source."
    ),
    checkpointer=InMemorySaver(),
)

response = agent.invoke(
    {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is Apple's 2025 revenue?"}]},
    config={"configurable": {"thread_id": "session-1"}},
)
print(response["messages"][-1].content)
```

**Agent reasoning flow:**
1. Query arrives
2. Call `get_filter_context` if query mentions specific company/year/type
3. Use returned context to decide `filters` dict
4. Call `search_documents(query, filters=filters)`
5. Answer from retrieved chunks

---

## Custom Metadata (Non-Financial Documents)

For non-financial domains (legal, HR, medical, technical), create a `metadata.yaml`:

```yaml
fields:
  - name: organization
    description: "Organization name in lowercase"

  - name: doc_type
    description: "Type of document"
    values: ["contract", "policy", "report", "memo", "other"]

  - name: effective_year
    description: "Year the document is effective, as integer or null"

  - name: jurisdiction
    description: "Country or region the document applies to, or null"

# Optional: override auto-generated prompt (must include {content} placeholder)
# prompt_template: |
#   Extract metadata from the document. Return ONLY valid JSON:
#   {"organization": "...", "doc_type": "...", ...}
#   Document Text: {content}
#   Extracted Metadata (JSON only):
```

Then reference it in `config.yaml`:

```yaml
metadata:
  config_file: "metadata.yaml"
```

Custom fields replace the default financial fields (`company_name`, `doc_type`, `fiscal_quarter`, `fiscal_year`). All file-level and chunk-level fields are still added automatically.

---

## Component API (Advanced / Custom Pipelines)

```python
from ragwire import (
    MarkItDownLoader, get_splitter, get_markdown_splitter, get_code_splitter,
    get_embedding, QdrantStore, MetadataExtractor, DocumentMetadata, FinancialMetadata,
    sha256_text, sha256_file_from_path, sha256_chunk,
    hybrid_search, mmr_search, get_retriever,
)

# Document loading
loader = MarkItDownLoader()
result = loader.load("document.pdf")
# result keys: success (bool), text_content (str), file_name (str), file_type (str), error (str|None)

# Text splitting
splitter = get_markdown_splitter(chunk_size=10000, chunk_overlap=2000)
splitter = get_splitter(chunk_size=10000, chunk_overlap=2000)        # recursive
splitter = get_code_splitter(chunk_size=2000, chunk_overlap=200)     # code
chunks = splitter.split_text(result["text_content"])

# Embeddings — provider config dict
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "ollama", "model": "qwen3-embedding:0.6b", "base_url": "http://localhost:11434"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "openai", "model": "text-embedding-3-small"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "huggingface", "model_name": "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "google", "model": "models/embedding-001"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "fastembed", "model": "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"})

# Vector store
store = QdrantStore(config={"url": "http://localhost:6333"}, embedding=embedding)
store.set_collection("my_collection")
vectorstore = store.get_store()  # returns LangChain VectorStore

# Custom metadata extractor
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
llm = ChatOllama(model="qwen3.5:9b")
extractor = MetadataExtractor(llm)                        # uses built-in FinancialMetadata schema
extractor = MetadataExtractor.from_yaml(llm, "metadata.yaml")  # uses custom YAML schema
meta = extractor.extract(text)                             # → dict
metas = extractor.extract_batch([text1, text2])           # → list[dict]

# Hashing
file_hash = sha256_file_from_path("document.pdf")  # → str
chunk_hash = sha256_chunk(chunk_id, content)        # → str
text_hash  = sha256_text(content)                   # → str
```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Error | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| Qdrant connection refused | `docker run -p 6333:6333 qdrant/qdrant` |
| `markitdown[pdf]` missing | `pip install "markitdown[pdf]"` |
| Ollama model not found | `ollama pull <model-name>` |
| `fastembed` missing | `pip install fastembed` (needed for `use_sparse: true` / hybrid search) |
| Embedding dimension mismatch | Set `force_recreate: true` once, restart, then set back to `false` |
| Hybrid search returns dense-only results | `pip install fastembed` + set `use_sparse: true` in config |
| Filter returns no results | Values are lowercased — check with `rag.get_field_values("company_name")` |
| `fiscal_year` filter not matching | Pass as `int` not `str`: `{"fiscal_year": 2025}` not `{"fiscal_year": "2025"}` |
